source.language_note: Rulebook is in Japanese; numeric values are reproduced here in SI.

full_prompt: Design a single-body machined pneumatic distribution manifold for an collegiate robotics benchmark robot that receives regulated shop air and fans it out to eight solenoid-valve ports. The rulebook sets the maximum allowable working pressure at 0.60 MPa gauge and requires any pressure-bearing component to withstand at least 1.2 MPa (2x MAWP) without permanent deformation and 1.8 MPa (3x MAWP) as a burst limit. The manifold external envelope must fit within 160 mm (length) x 50 mm (width) x 30 mm (height); the inlet is a single G1/4 thread on one short face; the eight outlets are M5-threaded cross-drilled ports arranged as two rows of four along the top face, pitched 20 mm apart, with the outlet axis perpendicular to the top face; the internal trunk is a 6 mm diameter longitudinal bore connecting inlet to all outlets. The manifold is machined from 6061-T6 aluminium with Young's modulus 69 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield strength 276 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 310 MPa. Mounting is via two through-holes of 5.2 mm diameter (M5 clearance) on the bottom face, spaced 140 mm apart, each fully fixed. Load Case 1 (MAWP): apply 0.6 MPa internal pressure on every wetted wall simultaneously. Load Case 2 (Proof): apply 1.2 MPa internal pressure. Load Case 3 (Burst check): apply 1.8 MPa internal pressure. Load Case 4 (Mounting torque): apply 6 Nm tightening torque about each M5 bottom hole with internal pressure at 0.6 MPa. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) under LC1, maximum von Mises stress must stay below 92 MPa (276 MPa yield divided by 3, a standard pneumatic vessel margin) and maximum bulging of any external face must stay below 0.10 mm; (R2) under LC2, max von Mises must stay below 184 MPa (yield/1.5) and residual deformation upon unload must be zero (fully elastic); (R3) under LC3, max von Mises must stay below 276 MPa (yield) so no plastic collapse occurs before the 3x MAWP burst; (R4) under LC4, combined stress from internal pressure + bolt preload must stay below 184 MPa; (R5) total manifold mass must be less than or equal to 150 g so the robot weight budget is respected; (R6) a linear buckling check on the thin top wall between the two outlet rows must return a load factor >= 3 at 1.2 MPa. Primary verification: linear static structural FEA of all four cases plus a linear buckling eigenvalue analysis of LC2 on the top wall. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Output one STEP AP242 file of the manifold as a single solid body and a report listing manifold mass in g, max von Mises per case in MPa, max external face deformation under LC1 in mm, and the buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design a single-body machined pneumatic distribution manifold for a collegiate robotics benchmark robot, fanning regulated shop air from one G1/4 inlet to eight M5 cross-drilled outlets. MAWP 0.60 MPa, proof 1.2 MPa (2x), burst 1.8 MPa (3x).

Envelope: 160 x 50 x 30 mm machined 6061-T6 billet; 6 mm longitudinal internal trunk; outlets in two rows of four at 20 mm pitch on the top face; two M5 mounting holes on the bottom spaced 140 mm apart (fixed).

Factor regime: yield FoS 3 at MAWP (standard pneumatic-vessel margin), yield FoS 1.5 at proof, yield at burst; buckling eigenvalue >= 3 on the thin top-wall strip between outlet rows at proof.

Load cases.
- MAWP: 0.6 MPa on all wetted walls.
- Proof: 1.2 MPa internal.
- Burst: 1.8 MPa internal (no plastic collapse).
- Mounting: 6 N-m torque on each M5 bolt combined with 0.6 MPa internal.

prompt.title: NHK robotics benchmark pneumatic distribution manifold
prompt.brief: Single-body 6061-T6 manifold with one G1/4 inlet and eight M5 outlets, sized and rated for the collegiate robotics benchmark 0.6 MPa pneumatic limit with 2x proof and 3x burst margins.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Not imposed by robotics benchmark rulebook.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Upstream regulator sets boundary; CFD unnecessary.

eval_coverage: buckling of thin wall between cross-drilled ports
